name: Sugar Twists
recipeIngredient: 1 and 1/2 cups Sugar (Can use less or more leftover sugar)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften yeast in warm water.
2. Sift flour and salt into a mixing bowl.
3. Cut butter and Crisco until the size of peas.
4. Blend eggs, sour cream, 1 tablespoon vanilla, yeast, and water together.
5. Add to dry mixture gently so that you keep the butter in little chunks.
6. Chill, wrapped in plastic wrap for two hours or up to 4 days.
7. Divide into two balls.
8. For each ball, roll out into a 6 x 12 inch rectangle.
9. Sprinkle with some of the vanilla sugar and fold in thirds.
10. Turn off and repeat this three or four times.
11. Roll out one more time and cut into 12 equal strips (1 X 6 inches).
12. Twist, roll in vanilla sugar, and put on a cookie sheet.
13. Bake at 375°F for 15 to 20 minutes. The twists should be lightly browned; do not overcook.
14. Enjoy!
Note: Do the second ball or save it for another day. The recipe makes about 24 twists.
